campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the business license tax rates that companies in town have to pay. Finance department staff Shannon Dragman presented the proposed changes to these tax rates, and the council discussed the matter publicly before deciding whether to approve the new rates.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates are being increased or adjusted
Finance department recommending specific rate changes
Council will vote on whether to approve the new tax ordinance
Changes would take effect in the upcoming fiscal year

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After listening to residents, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city law) and skipped the usual first reading process to speed things up. The ordinance is scheduled to be voted on and potentially approved at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback on a proposed ordinance
Council waived standard first reading procedures to expedite the process
Ordinance scheduled for final vote and adoption at next regular meeting
Community input considered before moving forward with new city rule

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from their previous session and review the city's finances for February. The council also authorized the Town Manager to sign several construction and professional services contracts, including work with QLM, Inc., and approved a resolution related to town improvements.
keyTopics: Approved February 2026 financial and investment reports
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other firms
Signed off on a three-year professional services agreement
Passed resolution for town infrastructure improvements

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 29, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met on April 29, 2026 to organize their upcoming meeting schedules. They reviewed and finalized the agenda for their May 5th meeting, decided which items needed specific time slots, and then looked ahead to plan the May 12th meeting agenda. The council also reviewed public records from mid-to-late April.
keyTopics: Finalized May 5, 2026 council meeting agenda
Scheduled specific time slots for agenda items
Added new items to May 12, 2026 draft agenda
Reviewed public records from April 16-23, 2026
Waiver requirements for agenda items and documents

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 28, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to review a draft plan for improving the city's trees and wildlife habitats, and celebrated the Rengstorff Park Aquatics Center winning a state award for excellence. The council also recognized Fair Housing Month to promote equal housing access for all residents.
keyTopics: Draft biodiversity and urban forest plan up for review
Rengstorff Park Aquatics Center receives state excellence award
Fair Housing Month proclamation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for open positions on city boards and commissions. Residents had the opportunity to provide public comments both in person and online before the meeting adjourned.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
Public comment period (in-person and online options)
Filling vacant positions on city advisory boards

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held an Audit Committee meeting where residents had the opportunity to speak about city matters for up to three minutes each. The committee approved meeting minutes from January and reviewed how the city's retirement and health benefits trust funds performed in the first quarter of 2026.
keyTopics: Public comment period on any city matters
Approval of January 26 meeting minutes
Retirement and health benefits trust fund performance report (Q1 2026)

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, and to approve a new labor agreement with the city's firefighters. One item was withdrawn before the meeting.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic (oversized) signs permitted near Levi's Stadium for FIFA World Cup 2026 events
New firefighters' contract for 2026-2029 with Local 1171
Staff-withdrawn agenda item (details not specified)

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026, where they discussed approving the city's budget for fiscal year 2026-27, reapproving tax rules, and setting new fees for city services. The council also heard about efforts to address homelessness in Santa Clara County and reviewed upcoming meeting schedules.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget and development fees approval
Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
Updates to city user fees and service charges
Santa Clara County homelessness prevention plan
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 28, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss several community projects and programs. The main topics included improvements to the Foothills Nature Preserve, the future of the middle school athletics program, and the Parks and Recreation Department's plans for the next fiscal year.
keyTopics: Foothills Nature Preserve Improvements Project updates
Middle school athletics program management and direction
Parks and Recreation Commission work plan for 2027
Department reports and staff presentations
Ad hoc committee and liaison updates
